outlook: UX design roles remain in demand with 12,547 open positions, though the market has contracted sharply with openings down 35.3% from last year. The median salary sits at $111,000, reflecting the continued value companies place on user experience expertise despite the cooling market. AI tools like Figma AI, Galileo AI, and Uizard are beginning to automate portions of the design workflow, earning the role a moderate AI risk score of 35 out of 100. While AI is streamlining wireframing and prototyping tasks, the strategic thinking, user research, and complex problem-solving that define senior UX work remain distinctly human capabilities that keep experienced designers competitive in this evolving landscape.

skillPivots.headline: Master Figma AI workflow optimization
skillPivots.body: Become an expert in leveraging Figma AI's advanced prototyping and design system features to dramatically accelerate design processes. Position yourself as the specialist who can train teams on AI-enhanced design workflows and establish best practices for AI-human collaboration in product design.

skillPivots.headline: Transition to product strategy leadership
skillPivots.body: Leverage your UX foundation to move into product management or strategy roles where human judgment in market positioning and user behavior analysis remains critical. Focus on developing business acumen and stakeholder management skills that complement your design thinking expertise.

skillPivots.headline: Specialize in AI tool integration
skillPivots.body: Become the go-to expert for implementing and optimizing Galileo AI and Uizard in design teams, focusing on tool selection, workflow design, and quality control processes. Develop expertise in prompt engineering and AI output refinement to ensure these tools produce brand-consistent, user-centered designs.
